data.title: CRWV Stock Rallies After Meta Announces Expanded $21B Deal — All You Need To Know
data.description:
CoreWeave Inc. (CRWV) shares soared more than 8% in Thursday’s pre-market trade after the company announced an expanded $21 billion deal with Meta Platforms Inc. (META).

In a joint announcement, CoreWeave stated that it will provide AI cloud capacity to Meta through December 2032 for about $21 billion.

CoreWeave will allocate capacity for Meta across multiple locations and will include some of the initial deployments of Nvidia Corp.’s (NVDA) Vera Rubin platform.

Retail sentiment on Stocktwits about CoreWeave trended in the ‘neutral’ territory, while CRWV was the second most trending ticker on the platform at the time of writing.

Get updates to this story developing directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Luxury Industry Expected to Remain Polarized — Market Talk
data.description: Luxury companies could confirm a continuation of trends for most brands in the upcoming reporting season, J.P. Morgan's Chiara Battistini says. "In an already challenging and polarized luxury industry backdrop, we expect the uncertainty emerging from the situation in the Middle East to translate into further divergence of performance among luxury brands," the analyst writes in a note. Despite recent geopolitical disruption, Battistini expects strong growth at groups exposed to both jewelry and high-end customers, like Richemont and Brunello Cucinelli, which proved to be more resilient than some peers in past quarters. Conversely, the performance of leather goods labels will remain bumpier in the first quarter, with some still working on ramping up more compelling product newness, such as Dior and Gucci, she says. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Price Falls. It Needs This to Happen to Break $5,000. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Gold and silver prices were dropping on Thursday as concerns swirl around whether the cease-fire between the U.S. and Iran will hold.

Continuous gold futures were down 0.9% at around $4,736 an ounce early Thursday. Silver futures were falling 2% to $73.87 an ounce.

Precious metals were boosted by the announcement of a truce between the U.S. and Iran, raising hopes that central banks will resume interest-rate cuts and stop selling their gold reserves. However, it's not clear if the cease-fire will last as Iran looks to exert control over the Strait of Hormuz, crucial for the safe passage of 20% of the world's oil.

"Traders are watching how [gold] reacts around $4,800. If it can hold around here and then push higher, then this could encourage fresh buying. But there are concerns that the upside momentum may soon fade, particularly if the U.S. dollar manages to find a foothold," wrote David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation in a research note.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Markets steady after decline while stockpiles fall
data.description:
Asia fuel oil markets steadied on Thursday after a sharp decline in the previous session, while onshore stockpiles started to show signs of a sharper drawdown.

Onshore fuel oil inventories in Singapore fell to their lowest level in 10 weeks amid reduced imports from the Middle East, data showed on Thursday.

The inventories fell below average after holding above it for several weeks. Supply recovery from the Middle East remained uncertain.

Meanwhile, several trades emerged for high sulphur fuel oil (HSFO) at rangebound premiums from the previous day. The market structure for both HSFO and very low sulphur fuel oil (VLSFO) continued to weaken in backwardation at the prompt months.

Cracks rebounded slightly on Thursday. The 380-cst HSFO crack (FO380BRTCKMc1) rose to a premium of around $2.65 a ton at the close, while the VLSFO crack (LFO05SGBRTCMc1) climbed to about $16.25, according to LSEG data.

INVENTORY DATA

- Singapore's residual fuel stockpiles (STKRS-SIN) declined 7.4% to 21.72 million barrels (about 3.42 million metric tons) in the week to April 8, based on Enterprise Singapore data.

OTHER NEWS

- Oil prices rose 3% on Thursday as doubts over a fragile two-week Middle East ceasefire raised concerns that energy flows through the crucial Strait of Hormuz will remain restricted.

- Commodities trader Glencore and Taiwan's state refiner CPC have chartered a tanker each to load Middle Eastern crude for Asia, while vessels in the Gulf are preparing to exit via the Strait of Hormuz, a day after the U.S.-Iran ceasefire.

- China bought a record volume of crude oil from Brazil in March, bringing the South American nation's total monthly crude exports to their second-highest level on record, according to data released this week by the Brazilian government.

- Russia will see revenue from its biggest single oil tax double to $9 billion in April due to the oil and gas crisis triggered by the U.S. and Israeli attack on Iran, Reuters calculations showed on Thursday.

WINDOW TRADES

- 180-cst HSFO: One trade.

- 380-cst HSFO: Five trades.

- 0.5% VLSFO: No trade.

data.title: Diesel backwardation firms again; regrade at $15 premiums
data.description:
Asia's diesel markets recouped some of the previous day's losses on Thursday, with the market's backwardation structure firming, while jet fuel spot demand for May surfaced and regrade inched back up to wider premiums

Traders were hoping for supplies to flow out of the Strait of Hormuz over the next few days, with a fragile ceasefire ongoing, though fresh fixtures were not yet firm.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) regained some ground, rebounding to around $60 a barrel, as the paper market gained from the previous trading session.

Cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF) climbed back to slightly above $36 a barrel, reflecting the firmer market structure.

More jet fuel demand emerged for May, in line with earlier expectations, as Indonesia's Pertamina sought to buy around 1 million barrels of the fuel.

As of this week, some analysts were expecting jet fuel demand declines in Asia to hit up to 150,000 barrels per day for the whole of the second quarter.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) firmed back to $14.5 a barrel premiums as demand was more robust in comparison.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No gasoil deal, two jet fuel deals

INVENTORIES

- U.S. crude stocks rose to their highest level in almost three years last week, while fuel inventories declined on rising international demand, the Energy Information Administration said on Wednesday.

REFINERY NEWS

- Exxon Mobil Corp plans overhauls in the spring and at the end of this year at its 612,000 barrel-per-day Beaumont, Texas refinery, said people familiar with plant operations.

- A diesel hydrotreater and the control room for multiple hydrotreating units at Valero's 380,000 barrel-per-day (bpd) Port Arthur, Texas, refinery were destroyed by a March 23 explosion and fire, said three people familiar with plant operations.

NEWS

- Taiwan's state refiner CPC and trader Glencore have chartered tankers to load Middle Eastern crude for Asia, while at least two Chinese vessels are heading to the Strait of Hormuz to exit the Gulf, a day after the ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran war.

- Investors placed an approximately $950 million bet on oil prices falling just hours before the U.S. and Iran announced a ceasefire, the latest large wager on the direction of the world's most traded commodity ahead of a major policy announcement by President Donald Trump.

- Oil prices rose on Thursday as doubts over a fragile two-week Middle East ceasefire raised concerns that energy flows through the crucial Strait of Hormuz will remain restricted.

- The Strait of Hormuz is shut and Iran must open it without conditions and be held accountable for damages after attacks on facilities, United Arab Emirates state oil giant ADNOC's CEO said on Thursday.

data.title: Awqaf Dubai educational endowment assets reach $195.5mln in 2026
data.description:
Staff Writer

The Endowment and Minors’ Trust Foundation in Dubai (Awqaf Dubai) has announced that the value of educational endowment assets registered with the Foundation reached AED718 million in 2026, marking a 52 percent increase compared to the previous year. The assets, distributed across 52 endowments, are dedicated to supporting the education sector and empowering students from low-income families.

The portfolio comprises 24 real estate endowments valued at AED663 million, including buildings, villas, and retail units, in addition to 28 financial endowments valued at AED55 million, consisting of bonds and shares.

Proceeds from these assets are utilised to provide educational opportunities and school supplies in collaboration with 45 educational entities. In 2025, approximately 3,100 students enrolled in public schools and various universities in the UAE and abroad benefited from the initiative, with scholarships totalling AED5.9 million.

Ali Al Mutawa, Secretary-General of Awqaf Dubai, said that educational endowments represent a key pillar in supporting underprivileged students, enabling them to pursue their education and achieve their academic ambitions.

He added that the 52 percent growth in the value of endowment assets reflects Awqaf Dubai’s continued efforts to expand its support and ensure the sustainability of educational giving.

Al Mutawa noted that Awqaf Dubai continues to develop its educational endowment portfolio and expand the number of beneficiaries, supporting more students and reinforcing long-term social impact.

He also highlighted the vital role of endowment funds in empowering students to advance in their professional paths, contributing to the development of a sustainable and progressive society.

He commended the contributions of endowers and philanthropists, noting that investing in education is an investment in the UAE’s future.

Article Source: Emirates News Agency (WAM)

data.title: Amazon to stock Lilly's new weight-loss pill at US kiosks, offer same-day delivery
data.description:
By Amina Niasse

Amazon.com said on Thursday its pharmacy unit will stock Eli Lilly's new weight-loss pill at kiosks located at some of the company's primary care clinics and offer same-day delivery of the drug.

Amazon Pharmacy began delivering GLP-1 medications in 2021, but does not stock injectable versions of the drugs in kiosks because they need to be refrigerated.

No need for cold storage is "what allows for broader access and for them to be stored safely in a kiosk for dispensing," Tanvi Patel, a vice president at Amazon Pharmacy, said of the pills.

About half of customers in the U.S. have access to same-day delivery, and the company aims to expand that, Patel said. All customers receive their medications within four days.

Amazon Pharmacy fulfills prescriptions made through Eli Lilly's direct-to-consumer pharmacy platform LillyDirect and has been stocking Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill at its kiosks since January.

Patel said Amazon benefits from being Lilly's dispensing pharmacy because of the Indianapolis-based drugmaker's early direct-to-consumer focus.

Lilly said last week it would begin selling its newly approved Foundayo weight-loss pill directly to cash-pay customers through LillyDirect at $149 per month for the lowest dose.

Customers can also access Amazon's same-day delivery through Lilly's prescribing partners, such as WeightWatchers , said Patel.

Novo's rival Wegovy pill, which began selling in January, is currently stocked in five kiosks in California, where customers can pick up the drugs at Amazon's One Medical locations following a medical appointment.

Amazon last year announced the kiosks for selling widely used medications such as antibiotics, blood pressure medicines and asthma inhalers, saying the option would reduce barriers to access and limit shipping costs.

Patients without a membership to One Medical, Amazon's primary and urgent care business, can still book an appointment and use the kiosks. A one-year One Medical subscription fee costs $199.

As the kiosks are introduced beyond California, Patel said, their locations may expand to other providers or clinics, if allowed by state regulation.

Amazon invested over $4 billion in 2025 in an effort to triple its company-wide delivery options this year, targeting underserved small towns and rural areas. By the end of 2026, same-day delivery access should reach 4,500 locales, Patel said.

data.title: Yomiuri: Shionogi Inks Contract with U.S. Govt For Antibiotics Production
data.description:
By Tomohiko Kanai / Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Shionogi & Co. announced Wednesday that it has signed an agreement with the U.S. government to produce antibiotics in the United States. With this agreement, Shionogi will have its first manufacturing site in the United States.

The Japanese pharmaceutical company will receive up to 482 million dollars (about 76 billion yen) in funding from the U.S. government and aims to begin commercial production in 2029.

A U.S.-based group company of Shionogi received the contract through the U.S. Health and Human Services Department. It is part of a project aimed at promoting research and development and other activities on medical treatment against threats from chemical, biological and other types of agents.

Shionogi will locally manufacture cefiderocol, which is used to treat bacteria that are resistant to multiple antibiotics. The drug is currently imported from Japan to the United States. Shionogi is working to establish manufacturing and sales networks in various countries, including developing nations.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

data.title: Aergo Releases HPP Node v.3.9.8
data.description:
Aergo announced that HPP Node v.3.9.8 is now live. According to the post, the release includes updates related to Arbitrum Nitro v3.9.8 and ArbOS 51 (BoLD). The team added that the upgrade is not mandatory, but is recommended for external node operators.

Refer to the official tweet by AERGO:

Aergo (HPP)
@aergo_io

HPP Node v3.9.8 is now live!

Includes Arbitrum Nitro v3.9.8 and ArbOS 51 (BoLD) related updates. This is not a mandatory upgrade, but it is recommended for external node operators.https://t.co/bPs0nCDvZJ

More updates are coming soon, so stay tuned!

Apr 09, 2026

AERGO Info

Aergo is an open-source enterprise blockchain platform optimized for hybrid deployments, encompassing both permissioned and permissionless blockchain architectures. Conceived and implemented by Blocko, a notable South Korean blockchain tech company backed by Samsung, Aergo is tailored for enterprise applications, enabling robust infrastructure for DApps.

Designed for enterprise adaptability, Aergo’s core features include a SQL-supportive smart contract engine, facilitating data management and developer-friendly contract programming. The platform hosts diverse blockchain infrastructures: private chains employ a Proof-of-Authority consensus mechanism with the RAFT algorithm, while its public counterpart, the Aergo mainnet, relies on a Byzantine Fault-Tolerant Delegated Proof-of-Stake mechanism. A significant distinction of Aergo is its ability to pay top-tier cloud service providers, like prominent telecommunication entities, to host dedicated nodes for private blockchains.

data.title: BlackBerry Signals FY27 Revenue Expansion as QNX Strength Supports 4Q Profit
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

BlackBerry laid out its fiscal 2027 growth outlook after reporting a fourth-quarter profit, supported by robust revenue from its QNX real-time operating system segment.

The Canadian software company said Thursday that it expects revenue to grow to between $584 million and $611 million in fiscal 2027, up from $549.1 million, and ahead of analyst projections of $576 million, according to FactSet.

QNX, which supplies its real-time operating system used in vehicles and embedded systems, is expected to generate about $290 million to $307 million, while Secure Communications revenue is expected to come in at $270 million to $280 million.

Excluding exceptional items, adjusted earnings are expected to be between 15 cents and 19 cents, with analysts expecting 17 cents.

In its fourth quarter ended Feb. 28, BlackBerry posted net income of $24.3 million, or 4 cents a share, compared with a loss of $7.4 million, or 1 cent a share, in the comparable quarter a year ago.

Adjusted earnings came to 6 cents a share. According to FactSet, analysts were expecting 4 cents a share, which was between BlackBerry's guidance range of 3 cents to 5 cents, according to FactSet.

Total revenue rose 10% to $156 million, topping forecasts of $141.9 million.

In its QNX segment, there was a 20% rise in revenue to $78.7 million, while Secure Communications revenue grew 8% to $72.5 million.

For the first quarter, the company expects revenue of about $132 million to $140 million, topping expectations of $129.4 million. QNX is expected to generate between $60 million and $64 million, and Secure Communications about $66 million to $70 million.

Adjusted EPS is pegged at 2 cents to 3 cents, with analysts forecasting 3 cents a share.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Romania Q4 GDP Growth Unrevised At 0.2%
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - Romania's economic growth moderated as estimated earlier in the second release, the latest data from the statistical office showed on Thursday.

On an unadjusted basis, GDP rose 0.2 percent annually in the fourth quarter, slower than the 1.7 percent rise in the preceding three-month period. That was in line with the previous estimate published on March 6. Moreover, this was the weakest growth since early 2021.

The expenditure breakdown showed that final consumption expenditure of households rose 0.3 percent, while general government consumption decreased by 0.3 percent. Gross fixed capital formation advanced 1.0 percent, and change in inventories grew 0.2 percent.

Net foreign demand was not favorable, and it negatively contributed by 0.6 percent as imports grew faster than exports.

On a seasonally adjusted basis, the economy contracted 1.4 percent annually, reversing a 1.4 percent growth in the previous quarter.

Compared to the previous quarter, GDP fell 1.8 percent versus a 0.1 percent decline in the third quarter.

Separate official data showed that Romania's foreign trade deficit narrowed to EUR 2.4 billion in February from EUR 2.9 billion in January as exports climbed 1.1 percent from last year amid a 3.6 percent fall in imports.
